platform.description: Detected platform (e.g., jsPsych, PsyToolkit, lab.js, Gorilla, etc.) or empty if unknown

key.description: Static response key, or null if the key must be resolved dynamically via response_key_js

response_key_js.description: JS expression evaluated on the page that returns the correct key to press. Use when the key-stimulus mapping is randomized per participant (e.g., counterbalanced color-key assignments). The expression should read the current stimulus from the DOM and return the correct key string.

accuracy.description: Per-condition accuracy (0-1). Keys are condition names from stimuli.

omission_rate.description: Per-condition omission rate (0-1). Keys are condition names.

temporal_effects.description: Temporal effects on the RT series. Each effect is optional — enable only those supported by the literature for this task. Provide rationale citing published studies.

autocorrelation.description: Trial-to-trial autocorrelation in RT (1/f-like serial dependence).

fatigue_drift.description: Gradual RT slowing over the course of the session due to fatigue.

slope_ms_per_trial.description: Mean RT increase per trial (ms). Typical range 0.05–0.5.

post_error_slowing.description: RT slowing on the trial immediately following an error.

delta_ms.description: Mean RT increase (ms) after an error. Typical range 20–80.

condition_repetition.description: RT benefit when the same condition repeats on consecutive trials (repetition priming / switch cost).

repeat_benefit_ms.description: RT reduction (ms) when the same condition repeats. Typical range 10–50.

pink_noise.description: 1/f (pink) noise added to the RT series to reproduce long-range correlations observed in human RT data.

amplitude_ms.description: SD of the pink noise component (ms). Typical range 5–30.

post_interrupt_slowing.description: RT slowing on the trial immediately after a stop-signal or trial interrupt event.

delta_ms.description: Mean RT increase (ms) after a stop-signal trial. Typical range 20–60.

between_subject_jitter.description: Between-subject variability parameters. Controls how much each simulated session varies from the base parameters.

rt_mean_sd_ms.description: SD across participants in overall mean RT (ms). Typical range 20–80.

rt_condition_sd_ms.description: SD across participants in per-condition RT offsets (ms). Typical range 5–30.

sigma_tau_range.description: Uniform range [min, max] for sampling the ex-Gaussian tau (ms) per participant. Typical range [20, 120].

accuracy_sd.description: SD across participants in per-condition accuracy. Typical range 0.02–0.08.

omission_sd.description: SD across participants in omission rate. Typical range 0.005–0.03.

rationale.description: Justification for the chosen variability values, citing published individual-difference or reliability studies.

runtime.description: Runtime configuration for the bot executor. Controls phase detection, timing, advance behavior, and trial interrupt settings.

phase_detection.description: JavaScript expressions evaluated to detect the current task phase.

method.description: Detection method: dom_query for jsPsych/HTML tasks, js_eval for PsyToolkit/canvas tasks

complete.description: JS expression returning true when the task is finished

loading.description: JS expression returning true when on a loading/start screen

instructions.description: JS expression returning true when on an instruction page

attention_check.description: JS expression returning true when an attention check is displayed

feedback.description: JS expression returning true when a feedback screen is displayed

practice.description: JS expression returning true during practice trials

test.description: JS expression returning true during test trials (used as fallback)

timing.description: Timing parameters for the executor polling and waiting behavior.

max_no_stimulus_polls.description: Maximum polls with no stimulus before stopping. PsyToolkit tasks need ~2000, jsPsych tasks ~500.

completion_wait_ms.description: Milliseconds to wait after task completion for data saving. jsPsych tasks need ~35000 (server upload), PsyToolkit ~5000.

rt_cap_fraction.description: Fraction of max_response_time to cap sampled RTs at

response_window_js.description: JS expression returning true when the platform's response window is open and RT timer has started. Required for PsyToolkit tasks where stimulus detection fires before the readkey step (e.g., cued task switching detects the cue but the response window opens later with the target). Use psy_readkey.keys.includes(KEYCODE) where KEYCODE is the JS keyCode of one of the task's response keys (e.g., 66 for 'b', 78 for 'n'). Leave empty if stimulus detection already aligns with the response window.

advance_behavior.description: How to advance through instruction/feedback screens between blocks.

pre_keypress_js.description: JS to execute before keypresses (e.g., 'psy_expect_keyboard()' for PsyToolkit)

advance_keys.description: Keys to press to advance between blocks

exit_pager_key.description: Key to exit pager mode (e.g., 'q' for PsyToolkit)

advance_interval_polls.description: Press advance keys every N polls with no stimulus

feedback_selectors.description: CSS selectors for feedback/advance buttons to click

feedback_fallback_keys.description: Keys to press if no feedback button is found

trial_interrupt.description: Trial-level interrupt behavior. When detection_condition matches a stimulus condition, the executor polls for that stimulus during the RT wait and probabilistically suppresses the response.

detection_condition.description: Stimulus condition name that triggers mid-trial detection. Empty string for tasks without trial interrupts.

failure_rt_key.description: Distribution key for RTs when suppression fails and a response escapes

failure_rt_cap_fraction.description: Fraction of max_response_time to cap failed-suppression RTs

inhibit_wait_ms.description: Wait duration (ms) after successful response suppression

data_capture.description: How to extract the experiment's recorded data after completion.

expression.description: JS expression returning data as string (for js_expression method)

button_selector.description: CSS selector for data button (for button_click method)

result_selector.description: CSS selector for result element (for button_click method)

wait_ms.description: Wait after button click before reading result

attention_check.description: Attention check detection and response configuration.

detection_selector.description: CSS/JS selector to detect when an attention check is displayed

text_selector.description: CSS selector to read attention check prompt text

response_js.description: JS expression returning the correct key to press. This is the primary response mechanism — provide complete parsing logic here.
